name: Garlic-Stuffed Mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the fresh Parmesan cheese for this recipe.
2. Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
3. Clean mushrooms by removing stems and reserving them. Parboil the mushrooms in just enough water to cover for 3 minutes until barely cooked, then drain well and blot with paper towels. Set aside.
4. If mushroom stems are woody, trim away the tip. Chop stems coarsely.
5. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a skillet. Toss chopped mushroom stems and scallions. Sauté until tender and most of the juices have evaporated. Add pecans, bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese, and remaining tablespoon of butter. Stir until butter is melted and absorbed. Stir in garlic béchamel. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper.
6. Stuff each mushroom cap with the mixture, mounding it neatly over the top. Sprinkle each with some cayenne pepper.
7. Place stuffed mushrooms in a lightly oiled shallow baking dish. Bake for 10 minutes or until heated through. Serve immediately.
Garlic Béchamel:
1. Scald 2½ cups milk (more or less, depending on desired thickness).
2. Whisk 1 large egg at room temperature into a bowl.
3. Melt butter in a heavy saucepan and whisk in flour. Cook roux over low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly.
4. Whisk in scalded milk. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cook gently, stirring frequently for 10-15 minutes until thickened.
5. Add salt and pepper, then stir in garlic puree from 3 large roasted heads of garlic.
6. Remove from heat and whisk some sauce into the egg. Return the egg mixture back into the sauce, tasting and correcting seasonings as needed.
7. Store in refrigerator with plastic wrap placed directly on the surface of the sauce until ready to use. Thin with milk before using if necessary.
